author: mrdavtan
name: Non-record: SWA + Doc-Isolated Eval ablation (two negative findings)
blurb: Three controlled runs on fast hardware (44ms/step) testing SWA and doc-isolated eval independently. SWA does not improve int8 quantization under default warmdown (delta +0.0004 BPB, within noise). Doc-isolated eval hurts by 0.009 BPB at stride=64, contradicting the LoRA TTT finding at stride=256. The crossover depends on stride length: at stride=64 context quantity dominates, at stride=256+ context quality dominates.
changes_from_baseline: SWA: weight averaging during last 50% of training (float32 accumulation, sampled every 50 steps)
Doc-isolated eval: _find_docs() splits val tokens at BOS boundaries, sliding windows per document
notes: Both findings are negative. SWA may help with aggressive warmdown (WD=20000) or int6; doc-isolation may help at stride=256+ but hurts at stride=64.
